g.data-narration: Welcome to this comprehensive module on the Importance of Software Quality in modern software engineering.
Software quality is critical because poor quality causes system failures, security breaches, and financial losses worth millions.
Software quality is the fitness of a software product for use, meeting both specifications and user needs.
g.text: "The degree to which a software product meets

g.data-narration: The ISO 25010 standard defines eight quality characteristics: functional suitability, performance efficiency, compatibility, usability, reliability, security, maintainability, and portability.
The Cost of Quality has three components: prevention costs to stop defects, appraisal costs to find defects, and failure costs from defects escaping to production.
The cost curve shows that investing in prevention and appraisal reduces failure costs. The optimal point balances all three, as external failures are the most expensive.
g.text: • External failures: After delivery → Much MORE expensive!

g.data-narration: Software Quality Assurance is a process-oriented preventive approach, while testing is a product-oriented detective activity. SQA encompasses testing but includes much more.
g.text: • Includes: process definition, audits, continuous improvement
• Part of SQA but NOT synonymous with it

g.data-narration: The McCall Quality Model identifies eleven quality factors: correctness, reliability, efficiency, integrity, usability, maintainability, testability, flexibility, portability, reusability, and interoperability.
CMMI defines five maturity levels: Initial (ad-hoc), Managed (processes characterized by projects), Defined (processes characterized organization-wide), Quantitatively Managed, and Optimizing.
g.text: Organizations at higher levels have controlled processes

g.data-narration: Quality metrics include product metrics (code complexity), process metrics (defect density), and project metrics. Multiple metrics should be tracked together for meaningful analysis.
Defect density measures defects per thousand lines of code. The formula is DD = Number of Defects / Size in KLOC. Industry benchmark for good quality is 1-3 defects per KLOC.
g.text: Track trends over time, not single values!
Consider defect severity and customer impact, not just density

g.data-narration: Modern practices include continuous integration, continuous delivery, automated testing, and Test-Driven Development. DevOps extends quality to operations with infrastructure as code and monitoring.
g.text: Move quality activities earlier in development lifecycle → Defects become cheaper to fix!

g.data-narration: A banking system requires high quality across all dimensions: zero tolerance for financial errors, 99.99% availability, strong security, and usability for all customers.
Key takeaways: Quality is fitness for use per ISO 25010. Prevention is more cost-effective than detection. SQA is process-oriented while testing is detective. Track metrics over time.

g.text: ✓ Cost of Quality = Prevention + Appraisal + Failure
✓ SQA ≠ Testing (process vs product, preventive vs detective)
✓ Shift Left: Move quality activities earlier
✓ Track metrics over time, not single values

g.data-narration: For exams: Know the ISO 25010 eight characteristics, the cost of quality components, defect density formula, and the difference between SQA and testing.

g.text: QA is preventive/process, Testing is detective/product
Cost of Quality: Prevention, Appraisal, Failure
Shift Left = earlier quality activities = cheaper fixes
